name: Alaska (United States)

colors.symbolism: the evening sky, the sea, and the forget-me-not (state flower)
Alaska's gold-mining heritage and the rich gold of its future

description: A dark blue field with eight gold five-pointed stars: seven arranged as the Big Dipper in the centre, and a larger eighth star representing Polaris in the upper fly.
history: Designed in 1927 by 13-year-old Benny Benson, an Alaska Native (Aleut) student who won a territorial flag-design contest. Adopted by the Alaska Territorial Legislature on 2 May 1927 and retained without change after Alaska became a U.S. state on 3 January 1959.

features.symbolism: the evening sky, the blue of the sea and mountain lakes, and the wild flowers (the forget-me-not, Alaska's state flower) of Alaska
the constellation Ursa Major, symbolizing strength.
Polaris, representing Alaska as the northernmost state in the union and its future as a state.
